result.stdout:
Usage: Squidex.CLI.dll [command]

Commands:

  ai        Uses AI commands.
  apps      Manages apps.
  assets    Manages assets.
  backup    Manage backups.
  config    Manage configurations.
  contents  Manage contents.
  indexes   Manage indexes.
  info      Shows information about the CLI.
  log       Analyze request log.
  openlib   Openlibrary example.
  schemas   Manage schemas.
  sync      Synchronizes apps.
  twitter   Manage twitter.

Use "Squidex.CLI.dll [command] --help" for more information about a command.

Use "Squidex.CLI.dll [command] --help" for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Uses AI commands.

Usage: Squidex.CLI.dll ai [command]

Commands:

  generate-contents  Generates content items and the corresponding schema.

Use "Squidex.CLI.dll ai [command] --help" for more information about a command.

commands.payload:
Uses AI commands.

Usage: Squidex.CLI.dll ai [command]

Commands:

  generate-contents  Generates content items and the corresponding schema.

Use "Squidex.CLI.dll ai [command] --help" for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Generates content items and the corresponding schema.

Usage: Squidex.CLI.dll ai generate-contents [options] <description>

Arguments:

  description  <TEXT>
  The description of your content.

Options:

  --app                  <TEXT>
  The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

  --schemaName           <TEXT>
  The optional schema name to override the result.

  -k | --key             <TEXT>
  The api key.

  --no-schema
  Do not create the schema.

  --no-contents
  Do not create the schema.

  --images
  Indicates if images should be generated.

  --delete-schema
  Delete the previous schema, if it exists.

  --model                <TEXT>    [gpt-4-turbo]
  The OpenAI chat model

  --image-model          <TEXT>    [dall-e-2]
  The OpenAI image model

  --num-contents         <NUMBER>  [3]
  The number of content items to create.

  --num-attempts         <NUMBER>  [3]
  The number of attempts. If an error happens the CLI will try to fix it with the AI.

  --language (Multiple)  <TEXT>    [en]
  The supported languages.

  --execute
  Execture the result and do not describe it.

Use descriptions with the following syntax:
    - "The ten biggest countries with name, iso2code and area"

commands.payload:
Generates content items and the corresponding schema.

Usage: Squidex.CLI.dll ai generate-contents [options] <description>

Arguments:

  description  <TEXT>
  The description of your content.

Options:

  --app                  <TEXT>
  The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

  --schemaName           <TEXT>
  The optional schema name to override the result.

  -k | --key             <TEXT>
  The api key.

  --no-schema
  Do not create the schema.

  --no-contents
  Do not create the schema.

  --images
  Indicates if images should be generated.

  --delete-schema
  Delete the previous schema, if it exists.

  --model                <TEXT>    [gpt-4-turbo]
  The OpenAI chat model

  --image-model          <TEXT>    [dall-e-2]
  The OpenAI image model

  --num-contents         <NUMBER>  [3]
  The number of content items to create.

  --num-attempts         <NUMBER>  [3]
  The number of attempts. If an error happens the CLI will try to fix it with the AI.

  --language (Multiple)  <TEXT>    [en]
  The supported languages.

  --execute
  Execture the result and do not describe it.

Use descriptions with the following syntax:
    - "The ten biggest countries with name, iso2code and area"

commands.payload:

Use "Squidex.CLI.dll backup [command] --help" for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Create and download an backup.

Usage: Squidex.CLI.dll backup create [options] <file>

Arguments:

  file  <TEXT>
  The target file.

Options:

  --app                  <TEXT>
  The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

  --timeout              <NUMBER>  [30]
  The timeout to wait for the backup in minutes.

  --interval             <NUMBER>  [30]
  The query interval to test if the backup is ready in seconds.

  --deleteAfterDownload
  Defines if the created backup shall be deleted from app after the backup task is completed.

  --force
  Overwrites the file if it already exists.

result.stdout:
Add or update an app.

Usage: Squidex.CLI.dll config add [options] <app> <client-id> <client-secret>

Arguments:

  app            <TEXT>
  The name of the app.

  client-id      <TEXT>
  The client id.

  client-secret  <TEXT>
  The client secret.

Options:

  -u | --url                 <TEXT>
  The optional url to your squidex installation. Default: https://cloud.squidex.io.

  -l | --label               <TEXT>
  Optional label for this app.

  -c | --create
  Create the app if it does not exist (needs admin client).

  -i | --ignore-self-signed
  Ignores self signed certificates.

  --use
  Use the config.

  --header (Multiple)        <TEXT>
  Adds a custom header in the format (Key=Value)

commands.payload:
Manage contents.

Usage: Squidex.CLI.dll contents [command]

Commands:

  enrich-defaults  Enrich the content items with its defaults.
  export           Export the content for a schema.
  generate         Generates test data.
  import           Import the content to a schema.

Use "Squidex.CLI.dll contents [command] --help" for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Enrich the content items with its defaults.

Usage: Squidex.CLI.dll contents enrich-defaults [options] <schema>

Arguments:

  schema  <TEXT>
  The name of the schema.

Options:

  --app               <TEXT>
  The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

  -u | --unpublished
  Handle unpublished content.

  --required-fields
  Also enrich required fields.

commands.payload:

result.stdout:
Export the content for a schema.

Usage: Squidex.CLI.dll contents export [options] <schema>

Arguments:

  schema  <TEXT>
  The name of the schema.

Options:

  --app               <TEXT>
  The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

  --filter            <TEXT>
  Optional filter.

  --text              <TEXT>
  Optional full text query.

  --order             <TEXT>
  Optional ordering.

  --output            <TEXT>
  Optional file or folder name. Default: Schema name.

  --delimiter         <TEXT>            [;]
  The csv delimiter.

  -u | --unpublished
  Export unpublished content.

  -m | --multiple
  Creates one file per content.

  --fields            <TEXT>
  Comma separated list of fields. CSV only.

  --full
  Write full entities, not only data when exporting as CSV. Default: false.

  --format            <IMEXPORTFORMAT>
  Defines the output format.
  Allowed values: CSV, JSON, JSON_Separated

Use the following format to define fields for CSV:
    - id
    - createdBy
    - created
    - lastModifiedBy
    - lastModified
    - version
    - data.name (for invariant fields)
    - data.name.de (for localized fields)
    - name=data.name (for invariant fields with alias)
    - name (German)=data.name.de (for localized fields with alias)

commands.payload:
Export the content for a schema.

Usage: Squidex.CLI.dll contents export [options] <schema>

Arguments:

  schema  <TEXT>
  The name of the schema.

Options:

  --app               <TEXT>
  The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

  --filter            <TEXT>
  Optional filter.

  --text              <TEXT>
  Optional full text query.

  --order             <TEXT>
  Optional ordering.

  --output            <TEXT>
  Optional file or folder name. Default: Schema name.

  --delimiter         <TEXT>            [;]
  The csv delimiter.

  -u | --unpublished
  Export unpublished content.

  -m | --multiple
  Creates one file per content.

  --fields            <TEXT>
  Comma separated list of fields. CSV only.

  --full
  Write full entities, not only data when exporting as CSV. Default: false.

  --format            <IMEXPORTFORMAT>
  Defines the output format.
  Allowed values: CSV, JSON, JSON_Separated

Use the following format to define fields for CSV:
    - id
    - createdBy
    - created
    - lastModifiedBy
    - lastModified
    - version
    - data.name (for invariant fields)
    - data.name.de (for localized fields)
    - name=data.name (for invariant fields with alias)
    - name (German)=data.name.de (for localized fields with alias)

result.stdout:
Generates test data.

Usage: Squidex.CLI.dll contents generate [options] <schema>

Arguments:

  schema  <TEXT>
  The name of the schema.

Options:

  --app               <TEXT>
  The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

  -u | --unpublished
  Import unpublished content.

  -c | --count        <NUMBER>  [10]
  The number of items.

  --file              <TEXT>
  The optional path to the file.

commands.payload:
Generates test data.

Usage: Squidex.CLI.dll contents generate [options] <schema>

Arguments:

  schema  <TEXT>
  The name of the schema.

Options:

  --app               <TEXT>
  The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

  -c | --count        <NUMBER>  [10]
  The number of items.

  --file              <TEXT>

result.stdout:
Import the content to a schema.

Usage: Squidex.CLI.dll contents import [options] <schema> <file>

Arguments:

  schema  <TEXT>
  The name of the schema.

  file    <TEXT>
  The path to the file.

Options:

  --app               <TEXT>
  The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

  -u | --unpublished
  Import unpublished content.

  --fields            <TEXT>
  Comma separated list of fields to import.

  --delimiter         <TEXT>            [;]
  The csv delimiter.

  --key               <TEXT>
  The key field to use.

  --format            <IMEXPORTFORMAT>
  Defines the input format.
  Allowed values: CSV, JSON, JSON_Separated

Use the following format to define fields from the CSV/JSON file:
    - name (for invariant fields)
    - name=other(for invariant fields from another field)
    - name.de=name (for localized fields)

commands.payload:
Import the content to a schema.

Usage: Squidex.CLI.dll contents import [options] <schema> <file>

Arguments:

  schema  <TEXT>
  The name of the schema.

  file    <TEXT>
  The path to the file.

Options:

  --app               <TEXT>
  The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

  -u | --unpublished
  Import unpublished content.

  --fields            <TEXT>
  Comma separated list of fields to import.

  --delimiter         <TEXT>            [;]
  The csv delimiter.

  --key               <TEXT>
  The key field to use.

  --format            <IMEXPORTFORMAT>
  Defines the input format.
  Allowed values: CSV, JSON, JSON_Separated

Use the following format to define fields from the CSV/JSON file:
    - name (for invariant fields)
    - name=other(for invariant fields from another field)
    - name.de=name (for localized fields)

result.stdout:
Create a new index.

Usage: Squidex.CLI.dll indexes create [options] <name> <fields>

Arguments:

  name    <TEXT>
  The name of the schema.

  fields  <TEXT>
  The fields in the following format 'field1=ASC,field2=DESC'

Options:

  --app    <TEXT>
  The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

  --print
  Only print the request to see the fields to be created.

Arguments:

  The name of the schema.

Options:

  --app             <TEXT>
  The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

  -r | --with-refs

result.stdout:
Synchronizes apps.

Usage: Squidex.CLI.dll sync [command]

Commands:

  describe  Describe the synced folder.
  in        Imports the app from a folder.
  new       Creates a new folder with sample files how to create an app from json files.
  out       Exports the app to a folder.
  targets   List all targets.

Use "Squidex.CLI.dll sync [command] --help" for more information about a command.

result.stdout:
Imports the app from a folder.

Usage: Squidex.CLI.dll sync in [options] <folder>

Arguments:

  folder  <TEXT>
  The target folder to synchronize.

Options:

  --app                      <TEXT>
  The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

  -t | --targets (Multiple)  <TEXT>
  The targets to sync, e.g. 'sync in -t contents -t schemas'. Use 'sync targets' to view all targets.

  --language (Multiple)      <TEXT>
  The content language to synchronize.

  --content-action           <CONTENTACTION>
  Defines how to handle content.
  Allowed values: Upsert, UpsertPatch, Create, Update, Patch

  --delete
  Use this flag to also delete entities.

  --patch-content
  Make content updates as patch.

  --recreate
  Use this flag to also recreate entities.

  --skip-assets
  Use this flag to sync asset folders but not assets.

  --update-current-client
  Also update the client that is used during the sync process.

  --emulate
  Use this flag to not make any updates and to emulate the changes.

result.stdout:
Exports the app to a folder.

Usage: Squidex.CLI.dll sync out [options] <folder>

Arguments:

  folder  <TEXT>
  The target folder to synchronize.

Options:

  --app                      <TEXT>
  The name of the app. If not provided then app configured in currentApp gets used.

  -t | --targets (Multiple)  <TEXT>
  The targets to sync, e.g. 'sync out -t contents -t schemas'. Use 'sync targets' to view all targets.

  --stream-contents
  Use the new streaming API for contents.

  --max-age                  <TIMESPAN>
  Content & assets created or last modified within time span defined.

  --describe
  Create a README.md file.
